description: Valid agent team configuration for Claude Code (experimental)

orchestration.description: Agent teams are orchestrated via natural language. You describe the task and team structure, and Claude creates the team, spawns teammates, and coordinates work.
examples: Create an agent team to review PR #142 with security, performance, and testing reviewers
Spawn 3 teammates to research authentication, database, and API modules in parallel

description.description: Description of what the team does

members.description: Array of team members (auto-populated when teammates are spawned)

teammateMode.description: How teammates are displayed. Configured in settings.json as 'teammateMode' or via --teammate-mode flag.

validValues.in-process: All teammates run inside main terminal. Use Shift+Down to cycle. Works in any terminal.
validValues.tmux: Each teammate gets its own pane. Requires tmux or iTerm2 with it2 CLI.
validValues.auto: Uses split panes if already in tmux, otherwise in-process. This is the default.

teamComposition.description: Team structure is specified via natural language, not declarative config

examples.requireApproval: Require plan approval before they make any changes
examples.assignRoles: One focused on security, one on performance, one on testing

architecture.teamLead: The main Claude Code session that creates the team, spawns teammates, and coordinates work
architecture.teammates: Separate Claude Code instances that each work on assigned tasks
architecture.taskList: Shared list of work items that teammates claim and complete (pending → in progress → completed)
architecture.mailbox: Messaging system for direct communication between agents

permissions.description: Teammates start with the lead's permission settings. If lead uses --dangerously-skip-permissions, all teammates do too.
permissions.note: Individual teammate modes can be changed after spawning.

items: Experimental feature — API and behavior may change without notice
No session resumption with in-process teammates (/resume and /rewind don't restore them)
Task status can lag — teammates may fail to mark tasks as completed
Shutdown can be slow — teammates finish current request before stopping
One team per session — clean up current team before starting a new one
No nested teams — teammates cannot spawn their own teams
Lead is fixed — cannot promote a teammate or transfer leadership
Higher token consumption due to multiple concurrent agents
Split panes require tmux or iTerm2 (not supported in VS Code terminal, Windows Terminal, or Ghostty)

hooks.TeammateIdle: Runs when a teammate is about to go idle. Exit 2 sends feedback and keeps teammate working.
hooks.TaskCompleted: Runs when a task is being marked complete. Exit 2 prevents completion and sends feedback.

example.name: code-review-team
example.description: Team of agents for comprehensive code review
